reasoning: Enterobiasis (pinworm infection) is generally a mild, easily treatable parasitic infection (usually with medications like albendazole or mebendazole). There are no approved vaccines for enterobiasis in humans, and virtually no active research into developing one because the disease burden does not justify the immense cost of vaccine development, especially when cheap and effective treatments already exist. Since there are no scientific or clinical trials developing a pinworm vaccine, it is essentially impossible for a vaccine to be successfully developed, approved, and listed on the Wikipedia page for infectious diseases by any of the resolution dates provided (all within early 2026). The only scenario where the page might list a vaccine is through temporary vandalism, which is statistically rare to land precisely on the resolution date and survive without being reverted. Thus, the probability remains at the 2% floor for all periods.
